column4: Question to MGRC

Can MGRC provide a breakdown of current monthly ticket/service request volume by application area (Salesforce, Oracle Fusion, OCI, NexSTAR, integrations, etc.) and by priority level (P1-P4) for the trailing 12 months?

What is the current FTE count (internal and external/contractor) supporting each application area today? How many of these resources are expected to transition to the MSP or depart during the onboarding period?

For the Knowledge Transfer phase (May 18 - July 6), will MGRC's current support resources remain fully available to shadow and co-work with MSP staff during the entire transition period, or is there an expected ramp-down schedule?
column8: Coverage gap risk if staff leave before KT complete.

column4: The architecture shows 50+ integrations across Oracle Cloud, Salesforce, RecVue, and external systems. Can MGRC provide: (a) an inventory of integrations with stability status (stable/active development/recurring issues), (b) error/failure rates for top integrations, and (c) when integration issues arise, who owns first-level triage vs. middleware team vs. external vendor coordination?
column8: Integration ownership is major scope ambiguity. Row 94 (OIC Incident Management) shows NO ownership assignment.

column4: The RFP notes OCI effort is estimated at under 1,000 hours annually. The Specific Requirements show 15 categories for OCI alone. Does the 1,000-hour estimate cover all categories or only Operational Day-to-Day? Can MGRC provide similar estimates for Oracle Fusion and NexSTAR?
column8: Scope could be 2-3x higher than stated.

column4: What is the current onshore/offshore distribution of support resources? Does MGRC require dedicated named resources assigned exclusively to MGRC, or is a shared/pooled resource model with guaranteed response SLAs acceptable?
column8: Named resources = $500K+ annual pricing difference vs. pooled.

column4: Is the July 6 go-live date firm or is there potential for schedule adjustment? What contingency exists if the Oracle Fusion go-live extends past May 18?

As MGRC consolidates vendor relationships what is the expected timeline for transitioning work from exiting vendors to the selected MSP(s)? Will the MSP be responsible for any transition activities from other vendors?

Can MGRC characterize the current state of operational documentation for each application area? Specifically for NexSTAR (custom 24x7 app), Material Voucher (Java app in architecture), and RecVue (billing hub) - what documentation exists for technology stack, codebase, and integration patterns?
column8: Custom apps are highest KT risk.

column4: The Specific Requirements include separate sections for OIC and MuleSoft. Can MGRC clarify: (a) the strategic division of workloads between platforms, (b) expected ratio of MSP effort between them, and (c) whether there are plans to consolidate to a single platform?
column8: Dual platforms = dual skill sets = higher staffing cost.

column4: Can MGRC share the relative weighting or ranking of the evaluation criteria (expertise, completeness, customer service, governance, vendor strength, compliance, value/TCO)?
Does MGRC have a preference between a single MSP provider across all solution areas versus multiple providers with complementary coverage?
What role does MGRC envision AI and automation playing in managed services over the next 2-3 years? Are there specific areas where automation is a priority?
column8: Levels the field on AI positioning.

column4: For after-hours data pipeline monitoring (10 PM - 7 AM PT), what is the typical nightly volume of pipeline runs and frequency of interventions? Is coverage required 7 days/week including holidays, and is the expectation for active monitoring (dedicated shift) or on-call response?

For Oracle Fusion period-close activities (GL, AP, AR, Inventory, Costing), can MGRC provide monthly volumes and clarify the expected MSP role: executing close processes (running jobs, reconciliation) vs. providing on-call support? Are after-hours or weekend close activities expected?
column8: Close execution = dedicated resources. Support = much lower effort.

column4: Will the MSP be expected to coordinate with or manage relationships with third-party vendors (Oracle support, MuleSoft support, implementation partners)? If so can MGRC provide a list of current vendors and their support scope?
What is the expected timeline for migration from FreshService to ServiceNow? Will the MSP support both platforms during transition and play a role in the ServiceNow implementation itself?

For NexSTAR (custom application with 24x7 requirement), can MGRC provide documentation on technology stack, codebase language/framework, architecture diagrams, known technical debt, and deployment pipeline? Will the MSP have full source code access?

For Customer Hub (nopCommerce), can MGRC clarify the PCI compliance scope: (a) does it process/store credit card data directly or redirect to a payment processor, (b) is the MSP expected to maintain PCI certification or support MGRC's audit process, and (c) what is the expected vulnerability scanning cadence?
column8: SAQ-D vs SAQ-A = massive difference in audit effort.

column4: What is the current state of test automation for regression smoke and integration testing? Would MGRC be interested in the MSP introducing automation capabilities?
Does MGRC have existing SIEM/SOC capabilities or will the MSP be expected to provide security monitoring and incident response?
For critical systems (Oracle Fusion, NexSTAR, Customer Hub) what are the RTO/RPO targets? How often are DR tests conducted and will the MSP plan and run those drills?

RecVue sits between Salesforce and Oracle ERP as a billing hub. Can MGRC provide: (a) monthly billing cycle volume, (b) typical exception rate, and (c) whether RecVue is SaaS-hosted (vendor DR) or self-hosted (MSP DR responsibility)?
column8: Row 17 marks RecVue Day-to-Day as MSP Owned. Hosting affects DR scope.

column4: The architecture shows a Material Voucher (Java Application) connected to Oracle SCM Inventory. Is this application in scope for MSP support or retained by MGRC? If in scope, where is it hosted and who owns the source code?
column8: Custom Java app - need to know if Java developers required.

column4: The architecture shows Salesforce plus Oracle CRM on Demand and Nextstar. Are Oracle CRM on Demand and Nextstar being retired or will the MSP support all three platforms?
The SLA defines response time targets (15 min for P1, 60-120 min for P2). Are there also defined resolution time SLAs? If yes, what are target resolution times for each priority level?
column8: Response is achievable. Resolution depends on issue complexity.

column4: What are the consequences when SLA compliance falls below objectives (95% for P1, 90% for P2-P4)? Are there service credits, penalty clauses, or remediation requirements?
Can MGRC provide historical performance data (past 6-12 months) for Average Resolution Time and SLA Compliance Rate to help set realistic targets?
Can MGRC provide a calendar of compliance audits scheduled for the next 12 months and clarify which audits the MSP will support or lead evidence gathering for?
The General Requirements reference NIST, SOC2, HIPAA, PCI, GDPR, CMMC. Which frameworks are mandatory today vs. aspirational? Specifically, does MGRC process HIPAA data or require CMMC certification?
Does MGRC have a target budget range or not-to-exceed threshold for the MSP engagement?
Is MGRC open to pricing models that combine fixed operational support with variable capacity for enhancement and project work?
For multi-year pricing does MGRC expect flat pricing across Years 1-3 or is there flexibility in how pricing is structured year-over-year?

For items designated as Shared Responsibility: (a) can MGRC define what Shared means operationally (who initiates, executes, approves), (b) will RACI be finalized before contract execution or during Phase 1, and (c) how will scope disputes be resolved?
column8: 150+ of 183 requirements are Shared. Without RACI scope disputes are inevitable.

column4: For scope changes after contract execution (new integrations, acquisitions, upgrades), what is MGRC's change request process? Will these be priced separately or absorbed into base contract?
Are there pending acquisitions that would affect MSP scope within the first 12 months? How have M&A integrations historically been handled from a support perspective?
column8: M&A onboarding listed as service domain.
